title: Derris scandens
taxonomy: Derris scandens is classified as a species within the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Derris. The species Derris scandens is native to tropical Asia. It is a climbing legume and is known for its heart-shaped leaves. The plant is often used as a natural insecticide due to its rotenone content.
uses: Derris scandens, also known as the climbing derris, has been found to possess various medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant contains compounds that have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities. In some cultures, Derris scandens is used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of this plant.

side_effects: Derris scandens, a plant traditionally used in folk medicine, has been reported to have potential side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues when ingested. The plant's compounds can also cause phototoxicity, leading to skin sensitivity and increased risk of sunburn. In some cases, Derris scandens has been linked to kidney damage and other organ problems.
